column1: A Tornado Warning has been issued for parts of Manatee until Aug 04 11:45PM. Download the ABC Action News App for the latest weather information.

I come on here and see a vague post about a tornado warning. I’ve spent the last half hour looking for more info, since I am potentially in the path, and there is nothing. Not another mention anywhere of this warning. It’s so weird here. I lived in Arkansas for years and I guess I’m used to a lot more coverage when it comes to tornadoes. I’ve seen what they can do. They scare me almost more than the hurricanes.
Exactky! I lived in GA and OH and they gave count downs to cities in the path and named the towns in danger. I’ve been beyond frustrated with this tonight - terrible information regarding these.
i know its like where did everyone go
The post below was more specific, red lines circling zone.

Tornadoes in the alley are far more long-lasting and stronger than tornadoes in FL. A tornado here is almost always going to be EF0 or a low EF1, with an incredibly rare EF2. Your best resource during tornado warnings is going to be the NWS SPC. They'll have a bulletin describing where it was sighted, how it was sighted, and the direction it's heading. Denis in particular analyzes the radar and may pass on analyzing ones that are evidently non-factors when there's a lot going on. https://www.spc.noaa.gov/
if you live in Manatee County, you need to sign up for their severe weather alerts. I’ve been getting texts and alarms on my phone all night. Go to mymanatee.org to sign up for the phone alerts:)
